mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite 3-month PAYEMS decline, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1.3 million annualized units, indicating robust consumer and housing demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 12-month trend AND HOUST > 1,300 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 673,902 million and HOUST at 1,354 thousand as of 2023-04-01
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST strength, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 3.8%, signaling simultaneous supply-side weakness and labor market softening that reduces near-term earnings visibility and triggers equity selloffs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.9286 and UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2023-05-01
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or supply shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression on 20d return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month, signaling that monetary tightening is successfully reducing price pressures and allowing the Federal Reserve to pause or cut rates in the near term, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: disinflation phase following monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite CPI deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in disinflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive and accelerating quarter-over-quarter while real retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month growth, indicating broad-based demand expansion that supports equity earnings and valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,439.607 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-01-01
regime_hint: any regime with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations where both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth are positive, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts, signaling rapid labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% and PAYEMS at 155,655 thousand as of 2023-05-01
regime_hint: labor market shock or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint UNRATE spike and PAYEMS decline, or interaction term has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows month-over-month while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 4.5%, indicating that supply-side expansion is occurring in a supportive monetary environment that allows corporations to invest and grow, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.9286 and FEDFUNDS at 5.06% as of 2023-05-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint INDPRO growth and accommodative FEDFUNDS, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression on 5d return direction.
